source: DESIGN_EVIDENCE_LEDGER.jsonl (append order PRESERVED = the ordinal time axis; there is no ts field)
principle: MECHANICAL normalize rules only. No post-hoc semantic classification, no merging of near-terms, no category->value encoding.

PRIMARY.rule: verbatim field value -> str -> strip() -> upper(). Missing field -> 'MISSING'. NO merge of distinct verbatim values (e.g. AUDIT and CONSTRUCT_VALIDITY_AUDIT stay separate).

SECONDARY.rule: first underscore-delimited token of 'decision' -> strip() -> upper(). Missing -> 'MISSING'. First-token split is a purely mechanical tokenization, not a semantic grouping.
SECONDARY.status: REPORTED-ONLY (noisier tokens e.g. '6','H1','R4','B'); NOT in the primary decision

frozen_normalize_rules: 1. read rows in file append order; index i = ordinal position (0..N-1).
2. symbol = verbatim field (PRIMARY) or first '_' token (SECONDARY).
4. missing/None -> literal 'MISSING' (a distinct symbol, not dropped).
5. NO semantic merge, NO alias table, NO value assigned to any symbol.
6. spelling-variant check ran (canon = lower+alnum): 0 collisions -> no merge needed and none applied.
forbidden: known-incident position passed to any generator/detector
any post-hoc addition of a normalization rule after seeing salience results
